name: Capital Soleil UV-Age Daily SPF 50+
hero: A fluid built for melasma and PIH-prone skin: PPD ≈ 46, niacinamide 4%, and the lightest LRP-style cast in this price band. The pigment patient's everyday default.

scoreBreakdown.n: PPD ≈ 46 — top quartile in fluids. SPF tested in vivo.
Niacinamide 4% adds real anti-pigment value vs a plain SPF.
Light fluid; faint cast on deep skin tones, much less than mineral.
₹ 2,100 / 40 mL — premium but justified by formula depth.

ingredients.note: The new long-UVA standard. Outside US markets only.
Photostable. Stabilises the rest of the system.
Pure UVA workhorse. Pairs with Mexoryl for full curve.
Inhibits melanosome transfer at clinically active dose.
Modest pigment-bonus active. Real but not the headline.

useCases.b: Daily Mexoryl 400 + niacinamide is the closest off-the-shelf melasma defence.
Months 2-12 after pigment laser. Critical that PPD stays high.
Fluid finish, faint cast even on Type V/VI. Reapplies cleanly under makeup.

alts.name: Anthelios UVMune 400 SPF 50+
alts.note: Higher PPD; no niacinamide. Pure photoprotection champion.

alts.name: Photoderm Spot-Age SPF 50+
alts.note: Antioxidant-loaded; UVA modest. Pigment chase, soft.

faq.a: Yes — at 4%, it's at the dose proven to reduce melanosome transfer in 8-12 week trials. It won't replace a tranexamic acid serum, but it's a free anti-pigment bonus inside the SPF.

faq.q: How does it compare with UVMune 400?
faq.a: UVMune has a higher PPD and a slightly more elegant skin feel. UV-Age Daily adds the niacinamide bonus and a niacinamide-trained vehicle. Pigment-prone patients usually prefer UV-Age Daily; everyone else marginally prefers UVMune.

faq.q: Can I layer my vitamin C under it?
faq.a: Yes — let the C sink in for 5 minutes. The two are complementary, not redundant. C handles the small dose of UV that gets through; the SPF blocks the rest.
Less than mineral SPFs but more than UVMune. Apply, wait 90 seconds, then pat foundation — don't rub.

sources.n: Bissonnette R et al. Long-UVA protection of a new sunscreen filter. JEADV 2022.
Hakozaki T et al. Niacinamide in pigmentation: melanosome transfer inhibition. BJD 2002.
Vichy clinical dossier. Capital Soleil UV-Age in vivo PPD 46. 2023.
Passeron T et al. Sunscreens with biological filters in melasma. Pigm Cell Res 2018.
